commit.message:
fix: escape HTML in binder print template strings

Card names from the TCG API (e.g. Farfetch'd, Nidoran♀) and
other user-facing strings were interpolated directly into HTML
template strings in buildPrintHTML, causing special characters
to render as escape sequences in the print view.

Added escapeHtml() for all interpolated values: card.name,
card.pokemonName, card.imageUrl, card.setCode, and setName.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restructure: purpose-built agents with proper trigger routing

New agents:
  deployment-validator — triggered by Release pipeline (BuildSucceeded)
    Proactively checks health after every deploy, identifies which
    microservice was deployed, picks the right diagnostic skill.
  vm-ops-agent — triggered by Azure Monitor alerts (title contains 'disk')
    Handles VM infrastructure issues using disk-pressure-diagnosis skill.

Release triggers:
  Post-Deploy Validation → Release pipeline succeeds → deployment-validator
  Build Failure Investigation → Build pipeline fails → incident-handler

Incident filters:
  vm-disk-alert → title contains 'disk' → vm-ops-agent
  auto-investigate → all other Azure Monitor alerts → incident-handler

Release pipeline updated with 'services' parameter so agent can
read which microservices were deployed via GetPipelineRunVariables.

commit.message:
Fix Windows CI build failures by making spawn tests Unix-only

The tests for SpawnTool path resolution use 'ls' and absolute paths like
'/bin/bash' which are POSIX-specific and don't exist on Windows. These
tests were failing on the Windows CI runner.

Fixed by adding #[cfg(unix)] to both:
- test_spawn_validation
- test_spawn_path_resolution

This allows these tests to be skipped on Windows while still running on
Unix-like systems (Linux, macOS). All 82 tests pass locally.

[CORPUS] Manual PR ingestion: hydrator, normalizer, CLI command (#53)

  comments, commits, and diff concurrently via GitHub REST API; saves raw
  snapshots (pr.json, files.json, review-comments.json) to fixture raw/ dir;
  maps responses to domain models; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var for auth
- GitHubApiModels: internal DTOs for GitHub REST API response deserialization
- ManualSeedProvider (IDiscoveryProvider): converts static PR URL list to
  PullRequestCandidates; GetProviderName=manual, no incremental sync
- FixtureNormalizer: deterministic HydratedPullRequest -> FixtureMetadata;
  infers tags (async, contract-change, early-return, null-safety, state-mutation,
  logging, exception-flow, api-change) from diff content; detects dominant
  language from changed files; defaults tier to Discovery
- CorpusCommand + add-pr subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus add-pr --url <url>'
  hydrates a PR, normalizes it, saves metadata.json + SQLite index entry, and
  prints fixture ID and tags; --db and --fixtures flags for path overrides
- GauntletCI.Cli now references GauntletCI.Corpus
- corpus subcommand registered in Program.cs

fix: address PR review — default include_standard=True, use sub_search, guard higher-order torsions

  load_mm3_fld, and ForceField.from_mm3_fld. Loading standard MM3
  parameters is the expected behavior; callers that need substructure-
  only parsing can opt out with include_standard=False.
